_title: The body does not resist change. It resists unsafety.
_raw_content: You were never stuck. Your nervous system was never broken. It was doing the only thing it knows how to do — protecting you from what it once learned was dangerous. The problem is that it learned love was dangerous. Expansion was dangerous. Being seen was dangerous. So it kept you small and called it safety. Reprogramming is not about forcing yourself forward. It is about making the unfamiliar feel safe enough to stay.
slide_1_hook: You were never resistant to change — your body was resistant to unsafety.
slide_2: Your nervous system is not malfunctioning. It is executing old instructions with perfect precision. Every freeze. Every pull-back. Every self-sabotage. It learned, long ago, that something beautiful was followed by something unbearable. So it began to treat expansion itself as threat.
slide_3: You are allowed to stop fighting yourself. You are allowed to stop calling your protection a problem. It kept you alive. It did its job. But the contract is up now. You can thank it and still choose to rewrite it.
slide_4: The shift is not in pushing harder. It is in settling deeper. When your body feels safe in the new — not just your mind — the grip releases on its own. You do not break patterns. You outgrow them by making the unfamiliar home.
slide_5: Feel your feet. Feel the ground holding you without asking permission. That is what unconditional safety feels like. Your body already knows it. It is just remembering.
slide_6: I am safe in my expansion. I am safe in my becoming. My nervous system is learning that what is good for me is allowed to stay.
caption:
You do not have a resistance problem. You have a nervous system still running a safety protocol that was written when you were too young to choose.

It mapped love to loss. Visibility to punishment. Joy to the thing that comes after joy.

So every time you try to expand, your body pulls you back — not because you are broken, but because it believes the old world is still the current one.

Reprogramming is not war with yourself. It is the slow, patient act of proving to your own body that this time, the good thing gets to stay.

You do not force a new frequency. You make it safe enough to land.

That is the work.
